title: Ocean Impacts Dashboard
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Four synchronized Chart.js panels showing ocean temperature, pH, sea level, and coral bleaching trends from 1880-2025 with correlation overlay

learningObjectives: Compare trends in ocean temperature, pH, sea level, and coral bleaching events over time
Identify correlations between multiple ocean impact indicators
Analyze how increasing atmospheric CO2 drives interconnected changes in ocean systems
prerequisites: Basic understanding of climate change and the greenhouse effect
Ability to read and interpret line graphs
Introduction to ocean chemistry and the pH scale

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Multi-panel data exploration with synchronized visualization
keyQuestions: How are ocean temperature, pH, sea level, and coral bleaching events related?
When did the rate of change in ocean indicators appear to accelerate?
What mechanism connects rising CO2 to declining ocean pH?
Why do some ocean impacts lag behind others in their response to warming?
commonMisconceptions: Ocean acidification means the ocean is becoming acidic -- it is actually becoming less basic, shifting from pH ~8.2 toward ~8.1
Sea level rise is caused only by melting ice -- thermal expansion of warming water is also a major contributor
Coral bleaching is caused mainly by local pollution -- global ocean warming is the primary driver of mass bleaching events
assessmentOpportunities: Have students write an evidence brief citing specific data points from the dashboard
Ask students to predict future values of each indicator based on current trends
Use the normalized overlay to discuss correlation versus causation in climate data
